

$Pr(X ~~~~~~ \mathrm{and}~~~~~~~\phi ~~~~~~~~~ \mathrm{and}  ~~~~  \theta)$

$Pr({all ~the ~unknowns}  ~~~~  \mathrm{given} ~~~~~~~{what's ~known} )$

$Pr( ~~~\phi ~~~ \mathrm{and}  ~~  \theta~~~~~~~\mathrm{given} ~~~~~~~~~~~X)$

$\frac{Pr(X ~~~~~~ \mathrm{and}~~~~~~~\phi ~~~~~~~~~ \mathrm{and}  ~~~~  \theta)}{Pr(X)}$



(And beta equations)
$\color{blue}{\beta(1,1) = p^0 \times (1-p)^0 =~1~=~~ 'flat'}$

$\color{red}{\beta(2,2) = 6 p  (1-p) =~~ 'gentle ~ curve'}$


$\color{green}{= \frac{1}{2}}\times\color{blue}{\large{1}} ~~+ ~~\color{green}{\frac{1}{2}} \times \color{red}{\beta (2,2) }$
$\color{green}{= \frac{1}{2}}\times\color{blue}{\beta (1,1)} ~~+ ~~\color{green}{\frac{1}{2}} \times \color{red}{\beta (2,2) }$

$$
\begin{aligned}
probability~of~everything~
~&=~~~~~model ~~\times ~~~prior\\
&~=[X~|~\psi,\theta]~~\times~~~ [\psi,\theta]] 
\end{aligned}
$$

$$
\begin{aligned}
posterior~&=~[\psi,\theta~|~X]\\
~&=~\frac{model \times prior}{marginal~of~data}\\
&~=\frac{[X~|~\psi,\theta]\times [\psi,\theta]]} {[X]}
\end{aligned}
$$
The denominator just adds up over all the unknowns:
$$
\begin{aligned}
marginal~of~data~&=~ [X]\\
~&=~ {\large{\Sigma}\left( [X~|~\psi,\theta]\times [\psi,\theta]]\right)}
\end{aligned}
$$

Just sum out the unknown you don't care about.

$$
\begin{aligned}
posterior~&=~[\theta~|~X]\\
~&=~\frac {\large{\Sigma_\psi}\left(model \times prior\right)}{marginal~of~data}\\
~&=~\large{\Sigma_\psi}\left(\frac {model \times prior}{\left[X\right]}\right)
\end{aligned}
$$
You sum  $\Sigma_\psi$ over discrete don't-know-don't-cares,  
and you integrate $\int_\psi$ over continuous-valued don't-know-don't-cares.  

$$
\begin{matrix}
\mathrm{Think~ about}\begin{matrix} P(R|D)\\ P(R|L) \end{matrix} 
~~~
&\Rightarrow
&~~~~~~~\left\{ 
\mathrm{Prior~for~~ } 
\begin{matrix} logit(P(R|D))\\ logit(P(R|L)) \end{matrix} 
\right\} ~+~ Data\\
~\\
&&\Downarrow \mathrm{Calculate}\\
~\\
&&\left\{ 
\begin{matrix}
\mathrm{\\Posterior~for}
\end{matrix}
\begin{matrix} logit(P(R|D))\\ logit(P(R|L)) \end{matrix} 
\right\}

&&\Rightarrow  \mathrm{Undo~}logit
&&\Rightarrow 
\mathrm{Posterior~ for}\begin{matrix} P(R|D)\\ P(R|L) \end{matrix} 
\end{matrix}
$$


$$
\begin{pmatrix} logit(P(R|D)\\ logit(P(R|L) \end{pmatrix} 
\mathrm{is ~distributed~as} ~~ 
$$

$$
\mathcal{N} 
  \left( \begin{pmatrix}
    0 \\
    0
  \end{pmatrix} , 
    \begin{pmatrix}
    \tau+\phi  & \tau\\
    \tau & \tau+\phi\\
  \end{pmatrix} 
  \right)
$$
Here, the two zeros are $logit(0.5)$, or generally $logit(\mu)$.

(Besides mathcal, there's also fonts \usepackage[mathscr]{euscript} and \usepackage{mathrsfs})
